nameMeanings.name: Margareta
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margrethe
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . en.lifeSummary: When Margareta Sverkersdotter was born in 1192, in Sweden, her father, Kung Sverker den yngre Karlsson, was 29 and her mother, Benedikta Ebbesdotter Hvide, was 26. She had at least 5 sons and 1 daughter with Wizlaw I Fürst von Rügen. She died on 5 March 1232, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margareta Sverkersdotter est née en 1192, à Suède, son père, Kung Sverker den yngre Karlsson, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Benedikta Ebbesdotter Hvide, avait 26 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille avec Wizlaw I Fürst von Rügen. Elle est décédée le 5 mars 1232, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margareta Sverkersdotter 1192 in Schweden geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Kung Sverker den yngre Karlsson 29 und ihre Mutter Benedikta Ebbesdotter Hvide 26. Sie hatte mit Wizlaw I Fürst von Rügen mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 5. März 1232 im Alter von 40 Jahren.

Lei avuto almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia con Wizlaw I Fürst von Rügen. Morì il 5 marzo 1232, all'età di 40 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margareta Sverkersdotter(이)가 1192년 스웨덴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Kung Sverker den yngre Karlsson(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Benedikta Ebbesdotter Hvide(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Wizlaw I Fürst von Rügen(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1232년 3월 5일 40세의 나이로 사망했다.
